summary: Backend-only testing for Life Blueprint. Created pytest suite covering all 8 feature areas: health, seeded content (recipes/quotes/podcasts/meditations/affirmations), workouts CRUD + logs, custom recipe CRUD, journal CRUD, events CRUD, life-goals CRUD, and 4 Claude Sonnet 4.5 AI endpoints. All 18/18 tests passed on first run (~31s). No _id leaks, no pork/bacon in any recipe or AI meal output, AI endpoints returned real Claude responses (no fallback).

minor.issue: DELETE returns 200 even when workout doesn't exist (idempotent by design, but no 404 for missing id). Not blocking.
DELETE does not return 404 for non-existent ids (delete_one silently no-ops). Minor.
@app.on_event('startup') is deprecated in FastAPI; consider lifespan context manager (non-blocking).

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 496 lines — acceptable now but approaching the 700-line split threshold; consider splitting routers (workouts, recipes, ai, content) as features grow.
AI endpoints gracefully fall back with 200+error key instead of 5xx — good UX, but frontend must render 'text' regardless and optionally surface 'error' to the user.
DELETE endpoints should return 404 when id is not found to help the frontend show accurate feedback (currently always 200).
seed_data runs on every startup with count check — idempotent and safe.
No MongoDB _id leak observed on any endpoint (all queries project {_id: 0}). Verified across 10+ endpoints.

CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' is accepted by Starlette but browsers will reject credentialed requests with wildcard. Fine for no-auth single user app, but flag for future auth work.
test_credentials: No auth — single-user app; EMERGENT_LLM_KEY present in /app/backend/.env and working with Claude Sonnet 4.5.
seed_data_creation: No seed data created by tests; all CRUD tests prefix records with 'TEST_' and clean up after themselves.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green (18/18). Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable as regression. For next iteration (likely frontend), use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. AI endpoints take ~3-8s each; budget timeout >=60s. Seeded data auto-populates on backend startup if collections are empty.
